member.summary: Each three integers is indices in the input, formating a single delaunay triangle.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.DelanuayIndices.HalfedgeIndices
member.summary: Each index in this edge is the index of the opposite half edge.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.DelanuayIndices.HullIndices
member.summary: The indices of the convex hull of the input.

member.summary: Computes a structure containg the indices of the delanuay

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.AmoebaOptimizer
member.summary: Variant of the Melder-Nead general purpose multi dimensional minimum optimizer.

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.Apollonius2d
member.summary: This class is used to compute circles that tangents other objects.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Apollonius2d.ComputeTanTanTan(IGS.Genamo.Geometry2d,IGS.Genamo.Geometry2d,IGS.Genamo.Geometry2d)
member.summary: This function solves the Apollonius problem, which is to compute all circles that tangent three other objects. The objects to tangent should be of type IPosition2d, ICircular2d or Line2d. If any other type of geometry is given, an exception will be thrown.
member.returns: >A list of new circles, which all tangents the objects e1, e2 and e3.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Apollonius2d.ComputeTanTanRadius(IGS.Genamo.Geometry2d,IGS.Genamo.Geometry2d,System.Double)
member.summary: Computes the circles with a specified radius that tangents 2 other objects. The objects to tangent should be of type IPosition2d, ICircular2d or a Line2d. If any other type of geometry is given, an exception will be thrown.
member.param: The radius of the circles computed.
member.returns: A list of new circles, which all tangents the objects e1 and e2.

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d
member.summary: Class describing a circular arc. This circular arc should not be seen as a part of a circle, but more lika a bent segment. Internally it is stored with two points and a bulge factor, which makes it possible to have an arc that equals a line segment. The bulge is defined as i=4*atan(bulge) where i is the arcs included angle. A positive bulge is a counter clockwise arc while a negative is a clockwise.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.#ctor
member.summary: Default constructor creates a straight line zero length arc at origo.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.#ctor(IGS.Genamo.Arc2d)
member.summary: Copy constructor.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.#ctor(IGS.Genamo.Point2d,IGS.Genamo.Point2d,System.Double)
member.summary: Creates an arc between two points with the specified bulge factor.
member.param: The start point of the arc.
The end point of the arc.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.#ctor(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Creates an arc between two points with the sepcified bulge factor.

member.summary: Gets the arcs length.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Bulge
member.summary: Gets or set the arcs bulge factor.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Linear
member.summary: Returns true if the arc equals a line segment (within epsilon tolerance of the bulge).

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.SweepAngle
member.summary: The included angle of the arc.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.SignedSweepAngle
member.summary: The included angle of the arc, signed. Positive for CCW arcs and negative for CW ars.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.IsOn(IGS.Genamo.Point2d,System.Double)
member.summary: Checks if a point is on (close to) the arc.
member.param: The point to be checked to be on the arc.
The maximum allowed distance between point and arc to be true
member.returns: true if the point is on the arc within the given tolerance.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Tangent(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Returns the tangent angle in radians on the arc where the point is. This is done by adding or subtracting 90 degrees from the angle between center of the arc and p, which means the point does not have to lie exactly on the arc.
member.param: The point to check for tangent at.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.StartTangent
member.summary: Gets the tangent angle at the arcs start point.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.EndTangent
member.summary: Gets the tangent angle at the arcs end point.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Direction
member.summary: Gets the direction of the arc. If it is equal to a line segment, Direction.Linear is returned.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.IsCirclePointOnArc(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Checks if a point that is on the circle the arc is a part of is actually included in the arc. If the arc equals a line segment, it returns false. This function is much faster than IsOn(...), but only works well for points that is known to be on/close to the circle.
member.param: The point on the circle. If the point is not on the circle, the result is undefined.
member.returns: true if the circle point is included in the arc.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.IsCirclePointOnArc(IGS.Genamo.Point2d,System.Double)
member.summary: Checks if a point that is on the circle the arc is a part of is actually included in the arc. If the arc equals a line segment, it returns true if the point is on the segment. This function is much faster than IsOn(...), but only works well for points that is known to be on/close to the circle (or on the infinite line).
member.param: The point on the circle. If the point is not on the circle, the result is undefined.
member.returns: true if the circle point is included in the arc.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Eval(System.Double,System.Double@)
member.summary: Returns a point on the arc given a parameter in the range 0.0 - 1.0 . If the parameter is less than 0.0 or larger than 1.0 the point returned is on the linear extension of the arcs end/start.
member.param: Set to the tangent angle at the point computed.
member.returns: The point at the given parameter.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Eval(System.Double)
member.summary: Returns a point on the arc given a parameter in the range 0.0 - 1.0 . If the parameter is less than 0.0 or larger than 1.0 the point returned is on the linear extension of the arcs end/start.
member.returns: The point at the given parameter.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Param(IGS.Genamo.Point2d,System.Double@)
member.summary: Computes a parameter in the range 0.0 - 1.0 where a point is on the arc.
member.param: The point to compute for. If the point is not on the arc, a parameter larger than 1.0 is returned (the 'parametrical distance').
Set to the tangent angle on the arc at the position of the point.
member.returns: A parameter in the range 0.0 - 1.0 .

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Param(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Computes a parameter in the range 0.0 - 1.0 where a point is on the arc.
member.param: The point to compute for. If the point is not on the arc the result is undefined.
member.returns: A parameter in the range 0.0 - 1.0 .

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.MidPoint
member.summary: Gets the point on the middle of the arc segment.

member.summary: Gets the angle between the arcs center and start point.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.EndAngle
member.summary: Gets the angle between the arcs center and end point.

member.summary: Checks if a point is inside the segment represented by this arc. If the arc equals a line segment, it always returns false.
member.param: The point to check for inclusion.
member.returns: true if the point is inside the arc segment.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Radius
member.summary: Gets or sets the arcs radius. Setting the radius moves the start and end points appropriately straight towards or away from the arc's center point. If the arc equals a line segment, double.PosetiveInfinity is returned.

member.summary: Rotates the arc in-place around origo.

member.param: The distance to offset the arc. If posetive, the arc is offsetted to the right, otherwise to the left.
member.returns: A new arc which is the offset of this arc.

member.summary: Same as offset except that if the arc "warps" around a new arc is created instead of a point.
member.param: The distance to offset the arc. If positive, the arc is offsetted to the right, otherwise to the left.
member.returns: A new arc which is the offset of this arc.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Offset2(System.Double)
member.summary: Same as offset except that if the arc "warps" around a new arc is created instead of a point.
member.param: The distance to offset the arc. If positive, the arc is offsetted to the right, otherwise to the left.
member.returns: A new arc which is the offset of this arc.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Clip(IGS.Genamo.Extents2d,System.Collections.Generic.List{IGS.Genamo.Point2d})
member.summary: Clips the arc to a specified clipping box. The result is a list of start- and endpoints defining arc segments which are completely inside the clip box. This result is appended to an existing list of points.
member.param: The array to append points of interest to.
member.returns: true if any portion of the arc is inside the box.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Clip(IGS.Genamo.Extents2d)
member.summary: Clips the arc to a specified clipping box. The result is a list of start- and endpoints defining arc segments which are completely inside the clip box. This result is returned in a new point list. The result list is zero-length if the arc does not intersect the clip box.
member.returns: An array with start/enpoints of subarcs. If the array is empty, the arc is not visible in the clip box.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.Extents
member.summary: Gets the minimum area axis aligned box covering the arc completely.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.SegmentHeight
member.summary: Gets the height of the segment this arc represents.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.SegmentArea
member.summary: Gets the area of the segment this arc represents.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.SectorArea
member.summary: Gets the area of the circle sector this arc represents.

The mode to use when flattening. See FlattenMode for details.

member.summary: Flattens the arc with 'inside' mode, with a given tolerance. Also checks that the angle between adjacent segments in not larger than maxDeltaAngle.

Mode to use when flattening. See FlattenMode for details.
If the first point in the approximation should be added to the result.
If the last point of the approximation should be added.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Arc2d.LeftDeflection
member.summary: Another way to define an arc. If the left_deflection is 0.0, the element is a segment, otherwise the element is an arc. It represents the signed distance from the middle of the chord to the middle of the arc, so left_deflection is positive if the arc goes clockwise.

member.summary: A decision function that us used to decide if an edge is to be added to the result of a boolean operation.
member.param: The set this edge belong to 0=set1 or 1=set2.
Every time a leftedge is passed this counter is increased and every time a rightedge is passed this counter is decreased.
Same as totcnt but only with edges from set1.
Same as totcnt but only with edges from set2.
member.returns: true if edge should be added false otherwise.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Calc.FindZerosOfFunction(IGS.Genamo.NumericFunctionDelegate,System.Double[],System.Object,System.Int64,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Numerically searches for zeroes of a given functions.
member.param: The function whose roots are to be found.

An array that will contain the roots found. Each entry of this array that contains another number than 0.0 is seen as an initial guess for the solution. The length of this array decides the maximum number of roots found.
Any data sent back to the callback function.
Maximum number of iterations. Don't choose this number to large if speed is crucial.
Relative convergence criterion on successive iterations.
Absolute convergence criterion on the function values.
The spread for multiple roots, that is, if |rt - root[i]| is less tan epsmr, where root[i] is a previously found root, then rt is replaced by rt + mrspr.
member.returns: The number of roots found.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Calc.SolveQuadric(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Double},System.Double)
member.summary: Solves the quadric equation c2*x^2 + c1 * x+ c0 = 0 for x.
member.param: An array where solutions are pushed.
If discriminant is closer to zero than this value, the equation has one solution (allowing for slighly negative discriminants)
member.returns: The number of solutions found (=number of numbers pushed to array)

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Calc.Brent(System.Double,System.Double,System.Func{System.Double,System.Double},System.Double@,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Computes a Brent-style root bracketing. Returns true on success, or false on failure. Failure can only happen if the function value at xmin and xmax has the same sign, and the function values at xmin/xmax is not within zero tolerance.
member.param: The x value for the root.
The iteration stops when this tolerance is reached. If never reached it stops after 1000 iterations.

If abs(funtion(xmin or xmax)) xmin/xmax is smaller than this value and same signs for xmin/xmax is sent, it is reported as success. This is to find roots close to 0 but with a small tolerance error. Set to negative to disable.

member.summary: Computes the barycentric weights of a point to the triangle A-B-C and put it in the weights u-v-w. If the triangle is degenerate, this function returns false and u,v,w is set to 1/3 (centroid). otherwise true is returned.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Circle2d.Split(System.Double,System.Double,IGS.Genamo.Arc2d@,IGS.Genamo.Arc2d@)
member.summary: Splits the circle in two arcs using two parameters. The parameters are in the range 0.0 - 2*PI, with parameter 0.0 beeing the right quadrant of the circle. If the two parameters is equal, two zero length arcs will be computed since an arc cannot have an included angle of 360 degrees.
member.param: The first parameter to split at.
The second parameter to split at.
The counter clockwise arc from parm1 to param2.
The counter clockwise arc from param2 to param1.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Circle2d.Split(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Double})
member.summary: Splits the arc in a list of parameters in the range 0.0-2*PI, with parameter 0.0 beeing the right quadrant point of circle. If all parameters are the same, the result is undefined. Note that there must be at least two parameters in the parameter array.
member.param: An arary of parameters to split at. It does not need to be sorted since this is done internally.
member.returns: A list of arcs on this circle circumference.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Circle2d.Split(System.Double[])
member.summary: Splits the arc in a list of parameters in the range 0.0-2*PI, with parameter 0.0 beeing the right quadrant point of circle. If all parameters are the same, the result is undefined. Note that there must be at least two parameters in the parameter array.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.ClosestPair3d.SegmentSegment(IGS.Genamo.Line3d,IGS.Genamo.Line3d)
member.summary: Computes the two points closest to each other on two line segments. Always returns a result, even if the segments are parallel and parametrically overlapping. member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.ClosestPoints2d.ArcArc(IGS.Genamo.Arc2d,IGS.Genamo.Arc2d,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Computes the closest points between two arc segments. The resulting lines startpoint will be on a1, and its endpoint on a2. 'checkZeroIntersect' should be true if it's possible the segments intersect (ie. check for zero distance) Setting it to false will speed up the computation.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.ClosestPoints2d.ArcSegment(IGS.Genamo.Arc2d,IGS.Genamo.Line2d,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Computes the closest points between an arc segment and a line segment. The resulting lines startpoint will be on arc, and its endpoint on line. 'checkZeroIntersect' should be true if it's possible the segments intersect (ie. check for zero distance) Setting it to false will speed up the computation.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.ClosestPoints2d.SegmentSegment(IGS.Genamo.Line2d,IGS.Genamo.Line2d,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Computes the closest points between two arc segments. The resulting lines startpoint will be on l1, and its endpoint on l2. 'checkZeroIntersect' should be true if it's possible the segments intersect (ie. check for zero distance) Setting it to false will speed up the computation.
member.param: Optional. The default value is true.If set to , then ; otherwise, .

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.CollisionGrid2d.#ctor(IGS.Genamo.Extents2d,System.Int32)
member.summary: Create an acceleration grid given an estimation of the number of adds to be performed. If size ~= the square root of the estimation, the number of rows and columns is distributed according to the aspect ratio of the grid extents, using the value of 'size' (multiplied by a magic number).

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.CollisionGrid2d.Add(IGS.Genamo.Poly2d)
member.summary: Add a reference to a poly to the grid. The poly may be open or closed, or a single point. Empty polys are ignored. Open polys are treated as thin walls. Closed CCW polys are treated as obstacles (filled contours). Closed CW polys are treated as holes. Note: modifying the added poly results in undefined behaviour! That is: to maximize performance, polys are NOT copied!

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.CollisionGrid2d.PolyCount
member.summary: The number of successfully added polygons. Note that empty polygons are ignored, but single-point-polygons are added.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.CollisionGrid2d.Overlaps(IGS.Genamo.Poly2d)
member.summary: Determines whether a given test poly overlaps any of the so-far added polys. Add() and Overlaps() calls may be interleaved. Overlaps() assumes that a) the test poly is CCW if it is closed b) the added hole polys lie inside contour polys.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.CollisionGrid2d.FromPolys(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{IGS.Genamo.Poly2d})
member.summary: Skapar en CollisionGrid2d utifrån givna polysar. Notera att polysarna tolkas som "solider" om de är CCW, och hål om de är CW! Även öppna polys stöds. Men indata får ju vara lite snygg annars går det inte!

member.summary: Various algorithms used to compute convex hulls

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.ConvexHull2d.Melkman(IGS.Genamo.Poly2d)
member.summary: Returns an array of integer indices of the polyline V. V has to be simple without bulget segments. Computation time is linear. Result will have ccw direction. The point with lowest index of V that is on hull will be the first point of the result.
member.param: A poly to compute hull for. Can be open but must be simple.
member.returns: Array of poly indices.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.ConvexHull2d.MelkmanPoly(IGS.Genamo.Poly2d)
member.summary: Returns the convex hull of a simple polygon. V has to be simple without bulget segments. Computation time is linear. Result will have ccw direction.
member.param: A poly to compute hull for. Can be open but must be simple.
member.returns: A new CCW poly representing the convex hull.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.ConvexHull2d.GrahamScan(IGS.Genamo.Region2d)
member.summary: Assumes r is vectorized

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.ConvexHull2d.GrahamScanOLD(System.Collections.Generic.List{IGS.Genamo.Point2d})
member.summary: The classical graham scan algorithm. Computes the points of a convex hull.
member.param: All points that should be included in the computation.
member.returns: A list of new points that (in order) forms the hull of the inpoints.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.ConvexHull2d.ConcaveParts(IGS.Genamo.Poly2d,IGS.Genamo.Poly2d)
member.summary: Extracts the concave parts of a convex hull. chull must be the convex hull of pl

member.summary: Calculates the minimum distance between a circle and a line.
member.returns: The distance between the objects.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Distance2d.CircleCircle(IGS.Genamo.ICircular2d,IGS.Genamo.ICircular2d)
member.summary: Computes the distance between two circles circumferences.

member.summary: Computes the distance between two line segments.

member.summary: Computes the distance between an circular arc and a line segment.

member.returns: The minimum distance between the arc and the line segment.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Distance2d.ArcArc(IGS.Genamo.Arc2d,IGS.Genamo.Arc2d,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Computes the closest points between two arc segments. The resulting lines startpoint will be on a1, and its endpoint on a2. 'checkZeroIntersect' should be true if it's possible the segments intersect (ie. check for zero distance) Setting it to false will speed up the computation.

member.summary: Computes the distance between two polylines.
member.param: The first poly to compute for.
The second poly to compute for.
member.returns: The minimum distance between the two polys.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Distance3d.TriangleLine(IGS.Genamo.Triangle3d,IGS.Genamo.Line3d)
member.summary: Distance between triangle outline and an infinite line.

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d
member.summary: The ellipse can represent either a full ellipse or an elliptical arc. The ellipse can be tilted in world space, using the angle of the major axis. Degenerate ellipses (one of the axis is zero length) is not supported and will most likely produce odd results and exceptions. The elliptical arc is always seen as counter clockwise and the major axis is always the longest axis of the ellipse. Constuctors will swap axes and angles so that this conditions are fullfilled. A word about our definition of elliptical parameters: an elliptical parameter is in range 0-2pi, where parameter 0 is the right quadrant point of the ellipse in the ellipses local coordinate system. When the ellipse is an arc, the paramteric range of this arc is a sub part of this parametric range, which means an arc can for example have startparameter=0.3 endparameter=0.8.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.#ctor(IGS.Genamo.Point2d,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Constructs a full axis aligned ellipse with the specified radiis. The largest radius will become the major axis.
member.param: The length of one of the ellipses axis.
The length of the other ellipse axis.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.#ctor(IGS.Genamo.Point2d,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Constructs a full ellipse given center, two radiis and a tiltangle in the plane. The largest radius will become the major axis.
member.param: The length of one of the ellipses axis.
The length of the other ellipse axis.
The tilt angle in the plane.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.#ctor(IGS.Genamo.Point2d,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Constructs a full ellipse given center, two radiis and a tiltangle in the plane. The largest radius will become the major axis.
member.param: The length of one of the ellipses axis.
The length of the other ellipse axis.
The tilt angle in the plane.
The arcs start angle measured from radius1 axis.
The arcs end angle measured from radius1 axis.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.#ctor(IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d)
member.summary: Copy constructor.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Normalize
member.summary: Makes sure the ellipse major axis is longer than the minor axis, and fixes the parameters as needed

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.IsArc
member.summary: Returns true if this is an elliptical arc, or false if this is a complete ellipse.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Length
member.summary: Gets the length of the elliptic object whith an max error of Calc.Epsilon.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.ComputeLength(System.Double)
member.summary: Usually, the Length property of the ellipse should be used. This function can be used in case a custom accuracy is wanted.
member.param: The tolerance to use in the numeric integral that computes this.
member.returns: The length of the ellipse/elliptic arc with max error of the given tolerance.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.MajorAxis
member.summary: Gets the major axis of the ellipse as a new vector.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.MinorAxis
member.summary: Gets the minor axis of the ellipse as a new vector.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.IsEllipsePointOnArc(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Checks if a point included in the full ellipse is on the elliptical arc by checking it's side of the startpoint-endpoint line. If this ellipse is a full ellipse this function always returns true.
member.param: The point on the ellipses circumference to check for inclusion on the arc segment.
member.returns: true if the point is on the elliptical arcs circumference.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.ParamToAngle(System.Double)
member.summary: Computes an angle in radians given an elliptical parameter for this ellipse.
member.param: The parameter to convert to an angle.
member.returns: An angle.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.AngleToParam(System.Double)
member.summary: Computes an elliptical parameter given an angle in radians.
member.param: The angle to convert to parameter.
member.returns: An elliptical parameter.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.UnitCircleMatrix
member.summary: Gets a new matrix that will transform this ellipse to a unit circle (that is, a circle at world origo with radius=1.0)

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.OrthoMatrix
member.summary: Gets a new matrix that will transform the ellipse to zero tilt angle at world origo.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Transform(IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d)
member.summary: Transforms the ellipse in-place with a matrix. Note that the result is undefined for matrices with non uniform scale.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.MajorRadius
member.summary: Gets the length of the major axis.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.MinorRadius
member.summary: Gets the length of the minor axis.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Start
member.summary: Gets a new point that is the start point of the elliptical arc. Gets a point at angle/parameter 0.0 if this is a full ellipse.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.End
member.summary: Gets a new point that is the end point of the elliptical arc. Gets a point at angle/parameter 0.0 if this is a full ellipse.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Eval(System.Double)
member.summary: Evaluates a point on the ellipse given a parameter. The parametric range for an ellipse is 0-2*PI. For an elliptical arc the start an end of the range corresponds to where it resides on the full ellipses circumference.
member.returns: A new point on the ellipses circumference.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.IsParamIncluded(System.Double)
member.summary: Checks if an elliptical parameter is included on the arc. If this is a full ellipse this function always returns true.
member.returns: true if the parameter corresponds to a point is on the arc, otherwise false.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Extents
member.summary: Gets the bounding box that encloses this ellipse/elliptical arc.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Foci(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Computes one of the two focii of the ellipse.
member.param: If this is true, the focus point on the posetive extent of major axis is computed. Otherwise the opposite point is computed.
member.returns: A focii point of the ellipse.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.StartAngle
member.summary: Gets the start angle of the elliptical arc. (That is the angle between the center point and the start point of the arc in the ellipses local coordinate system) If this ellipse is a full ellipse this function returns 0.0

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.EndAngle
member.summary: Gets the start angle of the elliptical arc. (That is the angle between the center point and the end point of the arc in the ellipses local coordinate system) If this ellipse is a full ellipse this function returns 2*Calc.PI

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.WriteAutoCADScript(System.IO.StreamWriter,System.Int32)
member.summary: Writes text data to a stream that is suitable to load into AutoCAD using the script command.

member.summary: Translates the ellipse in-place using a given delta x and delta y.

The distance to move in y.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Mirror(IGS.Genamo.Point2d,IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Mirrors the ellipse in-place over a line specified by two points.
member.param: The start point of the mirror line.
The end point of the mirror line.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Scale(System.Double)
member.summary: Scales the ellipse in-place using world origo as scaling center.
member.param: The factor to scale with. If factor larger than 1.0 will give a larger geometry.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Scale(System.Double,IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Scales the ellipse in-place using a specified scaling center.
member.param: The factor to scale with. If factor larger than 1.0 will give a larger geometry.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Rotate(System.Double)
member.summary: Rotates the ellipse in-place around world origo.

member.summary: Rotates the ellipse in-place around a given point.
member.param: The angle to rotate in radians.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.ToString
member.summary: Creates a new string suitable for debugging purposes.
member.returns: A string containing elliptical data.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.ToRegion(System.Double)
member.summary: Approximates the ellipse in a region representation. Since an ellipse cannot be exactly represented by a region, a tolerance is used to approximate the ellipse into a polygon with linear segments only.
member.param: The tolerance/maximum error in approximation of ellipse.
member.returns: A region approximating the ellipse.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.ClosestPoint(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Computes a point on the ellipse/elliptical arc that is as close as possible to another point.
member.param: The point to be as close as possible to.
member.returns: A point on the ellipse/elliptical arc that is as near as possible to the specified point.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Perpendicular(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Computes new points on the ellipses periferi, which are perpendicular to the ellipse with respect to a given point. The points are on the full ellipse, even if it is an arc.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Quadrant(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns a new point on the ellipse that is a point on one of the ellipses extremes in the ellipse local coordinate system. The point is always computed as if the ellipse is a full ellipse and not an arc.
member.param: An integer in the range 0-3. 0=right, 1=up, 2=left, 3=bottom. If this number is out of range the modulus of it is used.
member.returns: A point on the full ellipses circumference beeing one of the quadrant points.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.MidPoint
member.summary: Gets the point that is on the middle of the elliptical arc in the means of distance along its circumference. This function does some quite heavy computations and is not very fast. Since it's solved numerically it's not exact.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Distance(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Computes a distance from any point to the ellipse/elliptical arc.
member.param: The point to compute distance from.
member.returns: The minimum distance between the point and this object.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.CreateFromLength(IGS.Genamo.Point2d,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Approximates a new elliptical arc given a startangle and a length. If the length is larger than a full ellipse of the specified parameters, an exception will be thrown (numerical computations will never converge).
member.param: The center point of the elliptical arc.
Radius of one of the axis.
The start angle of elliptical arc.
The length of the elliptical arc.
The tilt angle in the plane of the ellipse.
member.returns: A new elliptical arc with the given parameters.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.TangentPoint(IGS.Genamo.Point2d,IGS.Genamo.Side)
member.summary: Computes a point on the ellipses circumference that is the enpoint of a line that tangents the ellipse.
member.param: The startpoint of the tangent line searched.
The side of ellipse the tangent point is at from the 'from-points' view.
member.returns: A point that is the endpoint of a line that tangents the ellipse or null if the requested tangent does not exist (that is, when the from point is inside the ellipse)

Set to the elliptical arc after the parameter.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d.Split(System.Double[])
member.summary: Splits the arc in sub-arcs given a list of elliptical parameters. The list of parameters do _not_ need to be sorted.

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.ShapeCluster
member.summary: Represents a cluster of shapes identified by spatial proximity.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.ShapeCluster.Id
member.summary: Cluster identifier, starting from 0.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.ShapeCluster.Pattern
member.summary: Detected pattern info, or null if not analyzed.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.ShapeCluster.Indices
member.summary: Indices of shapes in this cluster, referencing the original input list.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.ShapeCluster.Centroid
member.summary: Centroid of the cluster (average of member centroids).

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering
member.summary:
HDBSCAN-based spatial clustering for 2D shapes.
Given a set of Poly2d shapes, identifies groups of shapes that are spatially close together, without requiring the number of clusters to be specified in advance. Handles clusters of varying density.
Algorithm: Hierarchical Density-Based Spatial Clustering (HDBSCAN) 1. Compute mutual reachability distances between shape centroids 2. Build minimum spanning tree (Prim's algorithm) 3. Build single-linkage dendrogram 4. Condense dendrogram (collapse spurious splits) 5. Extract optimal flat clustering via excess-of-mass 6. Assign noise points to nearest cluster
Time complexity: O(n²) where n is the number of shapes. Suitable for up to ~5000 shapes.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.Cluster(System.Collections.Generic.List{IGS.Genamo.Poly2d},System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Clusters shapes by spatial proximity. Every shape is assigned to exactly one cluster.
member.param: Minimum number of shapes to form a cluster. Use -1 for automatic estimation. Larger values produce fewer, bigger clusters.
Controls density estimation. Lower values detect sparser clusters and produce less noise. Use -1 for automatic.
member.returns: A list of shape clusters, sorted by Id.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.Cluster(System.Collections.Generic.List{IGS.Genamo.Poly2d},IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Clusters shapes using pre-computed representative points (e.g. area centroids).
member.param: Representative point for each shape (must be same length as shapes).
member.returns: A list of shape clusters.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.ClusterPoints(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Clusters a set of 2D points using HDBSCAN. All points are assigned to a cluster (no noise).
member.returns: Cluster label for each point (0-based, contiguous).

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.ComputeDistances(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Computes the full pairwise Euclidean distance matrix.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.ComputeCoreDistances(System.Double[0:,0:],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Computes core distance for each point (distance to its k-th nearest neighbor). The core distance controls the "density estimate" around each point.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.BuildMST(System.Double[0:,0:],System.Double[],System.Int32,System.Int32[]@,System.Int32[]@,System.Double[]@)
member.summary: Builds MST on the mutual reachability graph using Prim's algorithm. Mutual reachability: d_mreach(a,b) = max(core(a), core(b), dist(a,b)) This smooths out density differences between clusters.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.BuildDendrogram(System.Int32[],System.Int32[],System.Double[],System.Int32,System.Int32[]@,System.Int32[]@,System.Double[]@,System.Int32[]@)
member.summary:
Builds a single-linkage dendrogram from sorted MST edges.
The dendrogram is stored as parallel arrays. Entry i represents internal node (n + i). Leaf nodes are 0..n-1 (individual points).
The root is node (2n - 2), which is entry (n - 2).

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.NodeSize(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32[])
member.summary: Returns the number of points in a dendrogram node subtree.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.CondenseAndExtract(System.Int32[],System.Int32[],System.Double[],System.Int32[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Condenses the dendrogram and extracts optimal clusters using HDBSCAN's excess-of-mass method.
Condensation: Walk the dendrogram top-down. At each split, if one side has fewer than minClusterSize points, those points "fall out" as noise and the cluster continues. Only splits where both sides are large enough create new child clusters.
Extraction: For each condensed cluster, compute stability = how long points stay in the cluster. Select the set of non-overlapping clusters that maximizes total stability.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.AssignSubtree(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32[],System.Int32[],System.Int32[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Assigns all leaf points in a dendrogram subtree to a condensed cluster.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.DeselectDescendants(System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Int32},System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Int32},System.Boolean[])
member.summary: Deselects all descendant clusters when a parent is selected.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.AssignNoise(System.Int32[],IGS.Genamo.Point2d[])
member.summary: Assigns noise points (label < 0) to the nearest non-noise cluster. If all points are noise, puts them all in cluster 0.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.Renumber(System.Int32[])
member.summary: Renumbers labels to be contiguous starting from 0.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.GeometricClustering.BuildClusters(System.Int32[],IGS.Genamo.Point2d[])
member.summary: Builds ShapeCluster objects from labels and centroids.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect2d.EllipseLine(IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d,IGS.Genamo.Line2d)
member.summary: Intersects an infinite line with an ellipse that might be an elliptical arc. If the ellipse is not an arc, there are always zero or two intersection points.
member.param: The infinite line to intersect with
The first intersection point. If only one intersection is found it's stored here.
member.returns: The number of intersections found (0-2)

member.summary: Computes the intersection points between an ellipse and a finite line segment.
member.param: The line segment to intersect with.
Set to the first intersection point or null if no such exists.
Set to the second intersection point or null if no such exists.
member.returns: The number of intersection points.

member.summary: Computes the parameters of intersection between two infinite lines. Parameters are in range 0-1 if the intersection is in the segment span.
member.param: Parameter on first line where it intersects the second.
Parameter on second line where it intersects the first.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect2d.CircleCircle(IGS.Genamo.ICircular2d,IGS.Genamo.ICircular2d)
member.summary: Intersects two circles. This function will return 0-2 intersection points. One Intersection point means the circles exactly touches each other within epsilon tolerance.
member.param: Set to first intersection point or null if no such exists.
Set to second intersection point or null if no such exists.
member.returns: The number of intersections. 0=no intersection. 1=touces each other in one point. 2=true intersection.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect2d.TangentCircleCircle(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,IGS.Genamo.Point2d@,IGS.Genamo.Point2d@)
member.summary: Computes the points of intersection between two circular objects taking doubles for circle geometry description.
member.param: Set to the first point of intersection or null if no such exists.
Set to the second point of intersection or null if no such exists.
member.returns: The number of intersections. 0 if the circles do not intersect, 1 if they touches in one point or 2 if there is a real intersection.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect2d.CircleLineParametric(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.summary: Computes the parameters on a line where it intersects a circular object using double data for input geometry.
member.param: The first intersection parameter of line. Parametric range is 0-1.
The second intersection parameter of line. Parametric range is 0-1.
member.returns: The number of intersections. 0 if the circles do not intersect, 1 if they touches in one point or 2 if there is a real intersection.

member.summary: Computes the points of intersection between two ellipses/elliptical arcs.

The second point of intersection or null if no such exists.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect2d.EllipseCircle(IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d,IGS.Genamo.ICircular2d)
member.summary: Computes the points of intersection between an ellipses/elliptical arc and a circular object. This is solved numerically and has a max error of epsilon radians from object centers.
member.param: The first point of intersection or null if no such exists.
The second point of intersection or null if no such exists.
The third point of intersection or null if no such exists.
The fourth point of intersection or null if no such exists.
member.returns: The number of intersections found (0-4).

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect2d.EllipseArc(IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d,IGS.Genamo.Arc2d)
member.summary: Computes the points of intersection between an ellipses/elliptical arc and an circular arc. This is solved numerically and has a max error of epsilon radians from object centers.
member.param: The first point of intersection or null if no such exists.
The second point of intersection or null if no such exists.
The third point of intersection or null if no such exists.
The fourth point of intersection or null if no such exists.
member.returns: The number of intersections found (0-4).

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect2d.PolyEllipse(IGS.Genamo.Poly2d,IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d)
member.summary: Appends point of intersection between a poly and an ellipses/elliptical arc. This is solved numerically and has an angular error of max epsilon radians from object centers where poly segments are arcs. Linear poly segments are intersected exactly against the ellipse.
member.param: The list to append intersection points to.
member.returns: The number of intersections found. This is the same as the number of points appended to the output list.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect2d.RegionEllipse(IGS.Genamo.Region2d,IGS.Genamo.Ellipse2d)
member.summary: Appends point of intersection between a region and an ellipses/elliptical arc. This is solved numerically and has an angular error of max epsilon radians from object centers where region segments are arcs. Linear poly segments are intersected exactly against the ellipse.
member.param: The list to append intersection points to.
member.returns: The number of intersections found. This is the same as the number of points appended to the output list.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect2d.PolySelfIntersectParametric(IGS.Genamo.Poly2d,System.Double)

member.summary: Determines if a quadrilateral intersects with a line segment by dividing the quad into two triangles.
member.param: The intersection point if an intersection exists; otherwise, null.
member.returns: True if the quad and segment intersect; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect3d.FurthestPoints(System.Collections.Generic.List{IGS.Genamo.Point3d})
member.summary: Returns a line with the two points furthest away from each other

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect3d.LineCylinderParametric(IGS.Genamo.Line3d,IGS.Genamo.Point3d,IGS.Genamo.Point3d,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.summary: Infinite cylinder

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect3d.SegmentSegment(IGS.Genamo.Line3d,IGS.Genamo.Line3d,IGS.Genamo.Point3d@,System.Double)
member.summary: Intersects two 3d segments. They are considered to intersect if the closest distance between them is smaller than 'tol'. The resulting intersection point is take form l1 (in case a tolerance gap between segs exists)

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Intersect3d.BentQuadLineParametric(IGS.Genamo.Quad3d,IGS.Genamo.Line3d,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.summary: Computes the parametric intersection points between a potentially non-planar (bent) quadrilateral and a line.
member.param: The quadrilateral to intersect with the line.
The line to test for intersection.
The first parametric intersection value on the line (normalized to [0,1]).
The second parametric intersection value on the line (normalized to [0,1]).
member.returns: The number of valid intersections found (0, 1, or 2).
member.remarks: This function handles bent/warped quadrilaterals by transforming the geometry into a coordinate system where the line is aligned with the z-axis, then solving the intersection algebraically. The parametric values are normalized by the line length, so t values represent positions along the line segment.

member.summary: Copy constructor.

member.summary: Computes a least squares fit line of a list of points, starting at x0 and ending at x1. The error minimized is the y-diff to line only (not real distance to line).

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.End
member.summary: Gets a reference to the end point or sets it to a new point. Set copies the point.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.MidPoint
member.summary: Gets a new point that lies exactly between the start and end points.

member.summary: Calculates the Y value given X. The line is considered infinite.
member.returns: Y value for X on infinite line. If line is horizontal DY=0 returns p1.y.

member.summary: Gets the x coordinate of the start point.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Y1

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.X2

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Y2
member.summary: Gets the y coordinate of the end point.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.DX
member.summary: Gets the signed horizontal distance from start point to end point.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.DY
member.summary: Gets the signed vertical distance from start point to end point.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Length
member.summary: Gets or sets the distance between the definition points. When setting the length the endpoint is altered and the angle is the same.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.ProjectVertical(System.Double,System.Double@)
member.summary: Computes the y position on a line for a given x. If the line is vertical, y parameter is not changed, and the function returns false.
member.param: Y position on line or unchanged if the line is vertical.
member.returns: true if a new y value could be computed, otherwise false (which means the line is vertical)

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.ProjectHorizontal(System.Double,System.Double@)
member.summary: Computes the x position on a line for a given y. If the line is horizontal, x parameter is not changed, and the function returns false.
member.param: X position on line or unchanged if the line is horizontal.
member.returns: true if a new x value could be computed, otherwise false (which means the line is horizontal)

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.SquaredLength
member.summary: Gets the squared length of this line. This is faster than computing the actual length of it.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Distance(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Computes the distance from a point to this line as a segment.
member.param: The point to compute distance from.
member.returns: A distance from the point to the line segment.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Param(IGS.Genamo.Point2d,System.Double@)
member.summary: Computes the parameter in the range 0-1 where the projection of a given point resides on the line segment. If the parameter is less than 0 or larger than 1 the point lies 'before' or 'after' the line. A tangent angle at the point is computed as well (which in this case is same as lines angle)
member.param: The tangent angle at the projected point (which in this case is same as lines angle).
member.returns: A parameter on the infinite line.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Param(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Computes the parameter in the range 0-1 where the projection of a given point resides on the line segment. If the parameter is less than 0 or larger than 1 the point lies 'before' or 'after' the line.
member.returns: A parameter on the infinite line.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Eval(System.Double,System.Double@)
member.summary: Computes a new point which is on this infinite line given a parameter in range 0-1. Smaller parameters than 0.0 or larger parameters than 1.0 works as well, and returns points out of the line segment. A tangent angle at the parameter is computed as well (which in this case is same as lines angle)
member.param: The tangent angle at the parameter (which in this case is same as lines angle)
member.returns: A new point on the line segment.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Eval(System.Double)
member.summary: Computes a new point which is on this infinite line given a parameter in range 0-1. Smaller parameters than 0.0 or larger parameters than 1.0 works as well, and returns points out of the line segment.
member.returns: A new point on the line segment.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.DistanceInfinite(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Computes the distance from a point to the line in a way assuming the line is infinite.
member.param: The point to compute distance from.
member.returns: The distance from the point to the infinite line.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.ClosestPoint(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Computes a new point which resides on this line as a segment, which is as close as possible to a given point.
member.param: The point to be as close as possible to.
member.returns: A new point on the line segment.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.ClosestPointInfinite(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Computes a new point which resides on this line as if it was infinite, which is as close as possible to a given point.
member.param: The point to be as close as possible to.
member.returns: A new point on the line segment.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Angle
member.summary: Computes the angle in radians of this line, that is the plane angle from start to end point.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Transform(IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d)
member.summary: Transforms this line in-place with a matrix.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Extents
member.summary: Gets a new bounding box for this line.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Scale(System.Double)
member.summary: Scales this line in-place with a given factor. Factors larger then 1 gives a longer line and smaller than 1 gives a shorter.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Scale(System.Double,IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Scales this line in-place with a given factor and a specified origo. Factors larger then 1 gives a longer line and smaller than 1 gives a shorter.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Rotate(System.Double)
member.summary: Rotates the line in-place around world origo with a given angle in radians.
member.param: The angle to rotate with in radians.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Rotate(System.Double,IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Rotates the line in-place around a given origo and a given angle in radians.
member.param: The angle to rotate with in radians.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Offset(System.Double)
member.summary: Returns a new offsetted line. Positive offset offsetts to the right of the segment, and negative to the left.
member.returns: A new line segment which is the offset of this one.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Offset(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Same as offset but you can set start offset and end offset individually

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.WriteAutoCADScript(System.IO.StreamWriter,System.Int32)
member.summary: Writes the object as an AutoCAD loadable script to a stream using a specified color

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.IsVertical(System.Double)
member.summary: Checks if the line is vertical with a given tolerance.
member.param: The maximum delta x for this line to be considered vertical.
member.returns: true if the line is vertical.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.IsVertical
member.summary: Checks if the line is vertical with epsilon tolerance.
member.returns: true if the line is vertical.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.IsHorizontal(System.Double)
member.summary: Checks if the line is horizontal with a given tolerance.
member.param: The maximum delta y for this line to be considered horizontal.
member.returns: true if the line is horizontal.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.IsHorizontal
member.summary: Checks if the line is horizontal with epsilon tolerance.
member.returns: true if the line is horizontal.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Side(IGS.Genamo.Point2d,System.Double)
member.summary: Checks the relative side of a point to this line. A tolerance decides how close the point can be to the line to be considered to be 'on' the line.
member.param: The point to check side of.
The tolerance. (Note that this tolerance is _not_ a distance, but a factor relative the lines length, use epsilon if unsure!)
member.returns: A member of the Side enumeration (Left,Right or On)

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.CreateFromArc(IGS.Genamo.Arc2d)
member.summary: Creates a new line that has start and end points that equals a given arc.
member.returns: A new line.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.ToString
member.summary: Returns a new string containig debug purpose info.
member.returns: A string with line data.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.ToRegion(System.Double)
member.summary: Creates a new region that has the same geometry as this line. A tolerance can be given but is not used in this case.
member.returns: A new region.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Split(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Double})
member.summary: Splits this line segment to multiple line segemnt using a list of parameters in the range 0-1. The list of parameters does _not_ need to be sorted. Multiple parameters that are the same will output zero length line segments.
member.param: List of parameters to split at. Parameters less than 0.0 or larger than 1.0 is ignored.
member.returns: A list of new line segments.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Split(System.Double[])
member.summary: Splits this line segment to multiple line segemnt using a list of parameters in the range 0-1. The list of parameters does _not_ need to be sorted. Multiple parameters that are the same will output zero length line segments.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Line2d.Split(System.Double,IGS.Genamo.Line2d@,IGS.Genamo.Line2d@)
member.summary: Splits the line segment at a given parameter in the range 0-1. If the parameter is less than zero or larger than 1, one of the lines will have zero length.
member.param: The sub-segment in the range p-1

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1
member.summary: General purpose linked list class.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new, empty linked list.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.Clear
member.summary: Empties the linked list.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.ToList
member.summary: Create a List out of this LinkList. Note: only references are added to the list, if T is a reference type! That is, no copies are made!

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.Find(`0)
member.summary: Searches the list for a node containing obj using ==.
member.returns: A reference to the node containing obj or null if not found.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.AddLast(`0)
member.summary: Adds an object last in the linked list.
member.param: The object to add to list.
member.returns: A linked list node containing the added object.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.AddFirst(`0)
member.summary: Adds an object first in the linked list.
member.param: The object to add to list.
member.returns: A linked list node containing the added object.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.EraseLast
member.summary: Removes the last node/object from the list. If the list is empty, an exception will be thrown.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.EraseFirst
member.summary: Removes the first node/object from the list. If the list is empty, an exception will be thrown.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.Erase(IGS.Genamo.LinkListNode{`0})
member.summary: Removes a node from the linked list and returns the new node that is in the same position as the one removed.
member.param: The node to erase, which has to be a node of this list.
member.returns: The node that is in the same place as the one removed, or null if the last node was erased.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.First
member.summary: Gets a reference to the first node in the list.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.Last
member.summary: Gets a reference to the last node in the list.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.Insert(IGS.Genamo.LinkListNode{`0},`0)
member.summary: Inserts an object before the another node. The newly created node is returned. If the 'before object' is null, the data object is added last in the list.
member.param: Where to insert. If null, the object is added last in list.
member.returns: The newly created linked list node.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.Reverse
member.summary: Reverses the list in-place.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.Sort
member.summary: Sorts the list using the IComparable interface of the objects.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.Sort(System.Comparison{`0})
member.summary: Sort the list unsing a comparison function.
member.param: The function that compares the objects. This is a standard .net comparison function.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.Count
member.summary: Returns the number of elements in the list.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.CutNode(IGS.Genamo.LinkListNode{`0})
member.summary: Removes a node from the list, keeping it's position pointers. It is then possible to re-insert it with PasteNode(), but use with care because the previous and next node of this one still has to exist!

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.PasteNode(IGS.Genamo.LinkListNode{`0})
member.summary: Re-inserts a node previously removed with CutNode(...)

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkList`1.GetEnumerator
member.summary: Returns an iterator that iterates through this LinkList.
member.returns: An IEnumerator object.

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.LinkListNode`1
member.summary: A node structure for a generic Genamo.LinkList

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.LinkListNode`1.Next
member.summary: Gets or sets the reference to the next node from this one. null is gotten if there is no next node.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.LinkListNode`1.Prev
member.summary: Gets or sets the reference to the previous node from this one. null is gotten if there is no previous node.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.LinkListNode`1.Data
member.summary: Returns the object this node references.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.LinkListNode`1.Advance(System.Int32)
member.summary: Computes the node that is n steps away from this and returns it. n can be negative to step backwards. If the resulting node is out of list, null is returned.
member.param: The number of steps to advance. Can be negative.
member.returns: The node n steps away, or null if out of list bounds.

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d
member.summary: Class of which objects represents affine transformatins in 2d space. The matrix public doubles is a 2*3 matrix that has the following structure: | m00=xaxis-x | m10=xaxis-y | | m01=yaxis-x | m11=yaxis-y | | m02=transx | m12=transy |

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d.m00
member.summary: x of X-axis

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d.m10
member.summary: y of Y-axis

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d.m01
member.summary: x of Y-axis

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d.m11
member.summary: y of Y-axis

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d.m12
member.summary: y translation

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d.m02
member.summary: x translation

member.summary: Copy constructor.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d.#ctor
member.summary: Default constructor, which creates an identity matrix.

member.summary: Constructor that directly sets the elements of the matrix.

member.summary: Modifies the matrix in-place so that it is a identiy matrix.

member.summary: Multiplies a matrix with another. Mathematically: result = b * a (Note: reverse order! For convenience when writing larger transforms)
member.returns: A product of the two matrices.

member.summary: Computes a new matrix which is a pure rotation matrix.

member.summary: Computes a new matrix that represents rotation around a given point.

member.returns: A new scaling matrix.

member.summary: Computes a new matrix which is a pure scaling matrix where x and y scale is the same.
member.param: The scale factor the matrix should represent. A scale >1.0 applied on an object gives a larger object.
member.returns: A new scaling matrix.

member.summary: Computes a new matrix which is a scaling matrix with a given origo.
member.param: The scale factor the matrix should represent. A scale >1.0 applied on an object gives a larger object.
The point used as origo point for scaling.
member.returns: A new scaling matrix.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d.CreateScaling(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Computes a new matrix which is a 'stretch' matrix,that is scaling in x and y might be diffrent.
member.param: The x scale factor the matrix should represent. A scale >1.0 applied on an object gives a larger object.
The y scale factor the matrix should represent. A scale >1.0 applied on an object gives a larger object.
member.returns: A new scaling matrix.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d.CreateScaling(System.Double,System.Double,IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Computes a new matrix which is a 'stretch' matrix using a given origo, that is scaling in x and y might be diffrent.
member.param: The x scale factor the matrix should represent. A scale >1.0 applied on an object gives a larger object.
The y scale factor the matrix should represent. A scale >1.0 applied on an object gives a larger object.
The point used as origo point for scaling.
member.returns: A new scaling matrix.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Matrix2d.CreateShearing(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Returns a new shear matrix, which has its x axis is tilted xshearRadians from local x axis. y axis is tilted yshearRadians from local y axis. The shearing radians are clamped to be between -89 to 89 degrees.

member.summary: Finds all overlapping boundingboxes (with an optional tolerance), calling a callback for each of them. Returns true if the scan was breaked (that is if a callback return true)

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.OverScan3d`1.FindOverlaps(IGS.Genamo.OverScanCallback{`0})
member.summary: Finds all overlapping boundingboxes (with an optional tolerance), calling a callback for each of them. Returns true if the scan was breaked (that is if a callback return true)

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.PatternType
member.summary: Classification of a detected geometric pattern.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternType.Scattered
member.summary: No clear pattern detected.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternType.Linear
member.summary: Points along a line or near-line.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternType.CartesianGrid
member.summary: Regular grid, possibly rotated.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternType.StaggeredGrid
member.summary: Brick/offset pattern — alternating row offset.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternType.HexagonalGrid
member.summary: Honeycomb / 120° lattice.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternType.Radial
member.summary: Rings and/or spokes around a center.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternType.RadialStaggered
member.summary: Staggered radial (alternating ring offsets).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternType.Concentric
member.summary: Pure concentric rings, no spoke structure.

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo
member.summary: Result of pattern detection for a set of shapes.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.Type
member.summary: The detected pattern type.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.Confidence
member.summary: Confidence score 0-1. Higher is better.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.BasisVector1
member.summary: Primary lattice direction (for grid types).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.BasisVector2
member.summary: Secondary lattice direction (for grid types).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.GridAngle
member.summary: Angle in radians between basis vectors.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.IsStaggered
member.summary: True if the grid has alternating row offsets.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.StaggerOffset
member.summary: Stagger offset as fraction of basis vector (0 to 0.5).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.Center
member.summary: Center point (for radial types).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.RingSpacing
member.summary: Spacing between rings (for radial types).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.RingCount
member.summary: Number of detected rings.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.SpokesPerRing
member.summary: Number of items per ring, or -1 if variable.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.SpokeAngleOffset
member.summary: Angular offset of first spoke in radians (for radial angular scoring).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.Regularity
member.summary: Coefficient of variation of nearest-neighbor distances. Lower = more regular.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.RotationAngle
member.summary: Rotation angle to align pattern with axes (radians).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.RowCount
member.summary: Number of rows detected in grid.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.ColCount
member.summary: Number of columns detected in grid.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.Indices
member.summary: Indices of shapes assigned to this pattern (referencing the input list).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.LatticeOrigin
member.summary: Origin point used for lattice fitting (for grid scoring).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.RingRadii
member.summary: Individual ring radii (for radial/concentric scoring).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.LineDirection
member.summary: Line direction unit vector (for linear scoring).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.LinePoint
member.summary: A point on the line (for linear scoring).

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternInfo.ScorePoint(IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Compute how well a point fits this pattern. Returns the spatial distance (residual) from the point to the nearest expected position in the pattern. Lower = better fit. Returns double.MaxValue for Scattered patterns.

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings
member.summary: Settings for the pattern detector.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings.Trace
F:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings.ToleranceFactor
member.summary: Tolerance multiplier for lattice/ring point matching. tolerance = ToleranceFactor * medianNNDistance. Default 0.35 (35% of typical spacing).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings.MinPatternCoverage
member.summary: Minimum fraction of cluster points a pattern must explain to be accepted. Default 0.3 (30%).

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings.RansacIterations
member.summary: Number of RANSAC iterations for arc/line detection.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings.MinPointsForDetection
member.summary: Minimum number of points required to try pattern detection at all. Below this, patterns are classified as Scattered.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings.MaxArcRadiusChordRatio
member.summary: Maximum radius/chord ratio for an arc pattern to be accepted. Default 10.0.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings.MinArcSweepAngle
member.summary: Minimum sweep angle (radians) for an arc pattern to be accepted. Default ~15 degrees.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings.ClusterGapMultiplier
member.summary: MST gap multiplier for spatial clustering. Default 2.5.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings.MinClusterSize
member.summary: Minimum cluster size. Smaller clusters → scattered. Default 3.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings.MinRingsForRadial
member.summary: Minimum number of rings required for radial pattern detection. Default 2.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings.MinPointsPerRing
member.summary: Minimum points per ring for a ring to be counted. Default 3.

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector
member.summary:
Detects geometric patterns (grids, arcs, radial, scattered) in sets of 2D shapes.
ARCHITECTURE: 1. Shape-aware MST clustering (Distance2d.PolyPoly + ExtentsExtents) 2. Per-cluster density sub-clustering (NN distance bimodality) 3. Per-density-group spatial re-clustering (centroid MST with smart fragment merge) 4. Per-group single-pass pattern CLASSIFICATION: - ALL detectors run on the group - Best-fitting type wins → ALL points assigned to that pattern - No iterative splitting — clustering handles separation 5. Small/singleton clusters → Scattered

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector.DetectPatterns(System.Collections.Generic.List{IGS.Genamo.Poly2d},IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings)
member.summary: Main entry point. Detects all patterns in the given shapes.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector.TryDensitySplit(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Int32},IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings)
member.summary: Checks if a cluster contains shapes at different density scales by analyzing the nearest-neighbor distance distribution. If the NN distances are bimodal (e.g., tight grid at 1mm + radial at 7mm), splits into separate groups so each can be detected independently. Returns null if no clear density split is found.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector.SpatialSubCluster(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Int32},IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings)
member.summary: Splits a group of shapes into spatially separated sub-groups using a lightweight centroid-based MST with jump detection. Returns the input as a single group if no clear spatial gap is found.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector.DetectPatternsInCluster(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Int32},IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Classify a group of shapes as a single pattern. After MST clustering → density split → spatial re-clustering, each group is expected to be ONE pattern. We run all detectors and pick the best-fitting type, assigning ALL points to it. No iterative splitting — clustering handles separation.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector.MSTCluster(System.Collections.Generic.List{IGS.Genamo.Poly2d},IGS.Genamo.Extents2d[],System.Int32,IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings)
member.summary: Clusters shapes using real contour-to-contour distances. Uses ExtentsExtents as fast lower bound to skip expensive PolyPoly calls.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector.TryDetectRadial(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Int32},IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings)
member.summary:
Detects radial / concentric ring patterns. Estimates the center, then identifies concentric rings via distance histogram, and verifies angular regularity within each ring.
This is crucial for patterns like drains, turbines, flanges etc. where shapes are arranged in concentric rings around a center point.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector.EstimateRadialCenter(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Int32)
member.summary:
Estimates the center of a radial pattern.
Primary: centroid of all points. For any roughly symmetric radial pattern (drains, turbines, flanges) the centroid IS the center.
Secondary: if centroid doesn't find rings, try circumcircle voting from nearby triplets (same-ring points give the exact center).

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector.TryCircumcircleCenter(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Int32,IGS.Genamo.Point2d,System.Double)
member.summary: Attempts to refine a radial center using circumcircle voting from nearby triplets. Only nearby points (likely same ring) are used as triplets. Returns null if insufficient data.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector.RefineRadialCenter(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Int32}},IGS.Genamo.Point2d)
member.summary: Refine center using iterative adjustment to minimize intra-ring distance variance.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector.ComputeRingVariance(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Int32}},System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Compute sum of intra-ring distance variance for a candidate center. Used by RefineRadialCenter.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector.TryDetectGrid(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Int32},IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings)
member.summary: Tries to detect a grid (regular, staggered, or hexagonal).

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PatternDetector.TryDetectArcOrLine(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[],System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Int32},IGS.Genamo.PatternDetectorSettings,System.Int32)
member.summary: Tries to detect a dominant arc or line using RANSAC.

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.Plane3d
member.summary: 3d plane class

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Plane3d.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new plane passing thru world origo and beeing parallel with the xy plane.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Plane3d.#ctor(IGS.Genamo.Point3d,IGS.Genamo.Vector3d)
member.summary: Creates a new plane passing thru a given origo and having a specified normal.
member.param: A point the plane should pass trough.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Plane3d.ParametricIntersect(IGS.Genamo.Point3d,IGS.Genamo.Point3d,System.Double@)
member.summary: Returns a parameter where the plane intersects the line given by two points. This parameter is 0.0-1.0 if the plane is between the points. If the intersection is out of this range a value smaller than 0.0 or larger than 1.0 is gotten.
member.param: The first point of the infinite line to intersect with this plane.
The second point of the infinite line to intersect with this plane
Set to the parameter of the intersection on the line.
member.returns: true if the line intersects this plane, otherwise false (which means the line is parallel with this plane).

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Plane3d.Intersect(IGS.Genamo.Line3d,IGS.Genamo.Point3d@)
member.summary: Returns a parameter where the plane intersects a line. This parameter is 0.0-1.0 if the plane is between the points. If the intersection is out of this range a value smaller than 0.0 or larger than 1.0 is gotten.
member.param: The line to intersect this plane with.
Set to the point of intersection between this plane.
member.returns: true if the line intersects this plane, otherwise false (which means the line is parallel with this plane).

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Plane3d.PointInPlane(IGS.Genamo.Point3d,System.Double)
member.summary: True if the point is on the plane, within a given tolerance.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Plane3d.Side(IGS.Genamo.Point3d,System.Double)
member.summary: Computes a side of a point relative the plane. If the point is on the same side as the normal points to, Side.Right is returned. If the point is within 'tol' tolerance on the plane (in a dot product) Side.On is returned. Else Side.Left is returned.

member.summary: Return this if any intersections was found.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PlaneSweepResult.NoIntersectionsFound
member.summary: Return this if any intersections was not found.

member.name: F:IGS.Genamo.PlaneSweepResult.StopSweep
member.summary: Combine this flag with any other to stop the sweep.

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.PlaneSweepDelegate
member.summary: Callback delegate used for testing object agains each other in a plane sweep.
member.param: User id of the first object.
User id of the second object.
Append any result data to this array.
member.returns: Should return a combination of PlaneSweepResult flags, see this enum for details.

member.name: T:IGS.Genamo.PlaneSweep2d
member.summary: A class that implements an algorithm to test overlapping objects to each other. Uses bounding box rejection to speed things up, which means the time complexity is output sensitive.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PlaneSweep2d.Add(IGS.Genamo.IExtents2d,System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds a geometry to the sweeporder. Do not alter the objects geometry between adding it here and sweeping because only a reference to the object is stored.
member.param: A user id of the object sent to the callback for object testing.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.PlaneSweep2d.BoxFuzz
member.summary: Gets or set the fuzz-factor. That is the extra distance that is added to the objects bounding boxes, which in turn is used to determinate if there is a potential collision/intersection between objects.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.PlaneSweep2d.Sweep(IGS.Genamo.PlaneSweepDelegate,System.Collections.Generic.List{IGS.Genamo.PlaneSweepIntersection}@)
member.summary: The sweep function tries to test as few objects for intersection as possible.
member.param: The callback function that actually handles the intersection logic.
True if any intersection was found, otherwise false

member.summary: Clear the objects added with Add(...)

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Point2d.CompareTo(System.Object)
member.summary: Implements the i comparable interface. The point with the smallest y is the smallest point. If y is same for the points, the one with smallest x is the smallest one. This is suitable for sorting points for sweep algorithms for example.
member.param: The point to compare this point with.
member.returns: -1 if this point is smaller, 1 if this point is larger and 0 if this point equals the other.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Poly2d.Init(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{IGS.Genamo.Point2d})

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Poly2d.#ctor(IGS.Genamo.Point2d[])
member.summary: Create an open poly from (copied) points. Same as creating an empty poly followed by Add():ing each point, but shorter.

member.name: P:IGS.Genamo.Poly2d.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets a reference to a PolyPoint2d in the poly or sets it to a copy of a given point.
member.param: The index of the point to get or set. This index is 'circular' which means -1 is the last point and NodeCount is the first

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Poly2d.ChangeStart(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a new poly with a changed start point.

member.name: M:IGS.Genamo.Poly2d.ShortestSeg
member.summary: Returns the length of the shortest segment
member.returns: The length of the shortest segment in the poly
